name: ESP32-C6-DevKitC-1
description: Entry-level Espressif devkit for the ESP32-C6 (single-core RISC-V with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5 LE and 802.15.4). Two 16-pin 2.54 mm headers, two USB-C ports, BOOT/RESET buttons and an addressable RGB LED.
notes: On-board WS2812 addressable RGB LED on GPIO8.
GPIO24-GPIO30 are used by the in-package SPI flash and are not broken out on the headers.
Two USB-C ports at the USB end: one on the on-chip USB Serial/JTAG (GPIO12/GPIO13), one on a USB-to-UART bridge wired to UART0 (GPIO16/GPIO17).
GPIO0-GPIO7 are LP (low-power/RTC-domain) GPIOs; ADC1 channels 0-6 map to GPIO0-GPIO6.
Pin order verified against the official ESP32-C6-DevKitC-1 v1.2 user guide on docs.espressif.com; board outline dimensions are approximate.

pins.warning: Strapping pin: must be high to enter download boot reliably; GPIO8 and GPIO9 both low at reset is invalid. Also drives the on-board WS2812 RGB LED.

pins.caps: DIGITAL_IN | DIGITAL_OUT | PWM | ADC | RTC | STRAPPING
pins.warning: Strapping pin (MTDI); sets the SDIO driving clock edge at reset, floating by default.

pins.caps: DIGITAL_IN | DIGITAL_OUT | PWM | ADC | RTC | STRAPPING
pins.warning: Strapping pin (MTMS); sets the SDIO sampling clock edge at reset, floating by default.
USB Serial/JTAG D-; repurposing as GPIO disables the on-board USB port and JTAG debugging.
USB Serial/JTAG D+; repurposing as GPIO disables the on-board USB port and JTAG debugging.
BOOT strapping pin; must be high at reset to boot from flash. The on-board BOOT button pulls it low.
Strapping pin; selects the JTAG signal source during early boot. No internal pull resistors.

pins.label: RX
pins.warning: UART0 RX (U0RXD); wired to the USB-to-UART bridge used for the serial console and flashing.

pins.label: TX
pins.warning: UART0 TX (U0TXD); wired to the USB-to-UART bridge used for the serial console and flashing.
